  - Clear structure (intro, themed body paragraphs, brief conclusion).
  - Cite sources inline by their URL where claims are made.
  - Be temporally accurate (mention relevant dates/years).
  - Do not invent facts beyond the evidence.

Research question: What are the key developments and challenges in solid-state battery technology for electric vehicles in 2024, including major company announcements and technical breakthroughs?

Content: Introducing Donut Battery: Solid-State EV Battery Revolution | Ari Tulla posted on the topic | LinkedIn Skip to main content Introducing Donut Battery: Solid-State EV Battery Revolution This title was summarized by AI from the post below. Ari Tulla 5mo Report this post Today Donut Lab launched the Donut Battery: the world’s first all-solid-state battery in production vehicles. ⚡ Why is this a big deal? This solves the biggest issues with electric vehicles: range, cost, charging speed, and dependence on rare-earth materials. Donut Battery is a better battery than current Lithium-Ion batteries: •⁠ ⁠400 Wh/kg energy density •⁠ ⁠Lower cost than lithium-ion •⁠ ⁠Five-minute full charge and designed for up to 100,000 cycles •⁠ ⁠⁠Made of globally abundant materials The first EV powered by Donut Battery's Verge TS Pro superbike is available to customers, and Donut Lab is working with multiple other OEMs. Donut Battery comes in various sizes and formats. It's suitable for use cases ranging from SUVs to supercars, from drones to heavy machinery, and even for stationary energy storage. Check the launch video below to learn more. https://lnkd.in/dvfCVg_D Kudos Marko Lehtimaki and Tuomo Lehtimäk

Content: Recent progress and challenges for manufacturing and operating solid-state batteries for electric vehicles | MRS Bulletin | Springer Nature Link Skip to main content Advertisement Recent progress and challenges for manufacturing and operating solid-state batteries for electric vehicles Materials for Electric Vehicles Published: 11 June 2024 Volume 49 , pages 717–729 ( 2024 ) Cite this article Save article View saved research MRS Bulletin Aims and scope Submit manuscript Abstract Solid-state batteries (SSBs) are expected to play an important role in vehicle electrification within the next decade. Recent advances in materials, interfacial design, and manufacturing have rapidly advanced SSB technologies toward commercialization. Many of these advances have been made possible in part by advanced characterization methods, which elucidated materials properties, interfacial behaviors, and degradation modes and their underlying causes. These insights have informed efforts to advance manufacturing and synthesis approaches and achieve improved performance. Title: Solid-State Battery Advancements, Challenges, and Industry Impacts
Snippet: Browse recent research findings on solid-state batteries, including key challenges to adoption, various alternative materials, and operando studies that offer new insights.
Content: Solid-State Battery Advancements, Challenges, and Industry Impacts | ACS Publications Chemistry Blog Energy Solid-State Battery Advancements, Challenges, and Industry Impacts ACS Publications Sep 4, 2025 4 min read Browse recent research findings on solid-state batteries, including key challenges to adoption, various alternative materials, and operando studies that offer new insights. As we continue to shift at a global level towards renewable integration and carbon reduction, it is no wonder that battery science continues to be one of the most pressing and prevalent research topics. Building on the insights from this editorial , this article presents recent research findings and discussions published in ACS journals and their broader industry impacts. We will examine key highlights in a joint Collection from Journal of the American Chemical Society ( JACS ) and ACS Energy Letters , including trending reviews and perspectives on the advantages of solid-state batteries over conventional lithium-ion batteries, key challenges to adoption, various alternative materials, and operando studies that offer new insights. It delves into the significant ongoing research, development activities, and partnerships among industry leaders like QuantumScape, Toyota, Solid Power, and Samsung SDI, which aim to surpass the limitations of traditional lithium-ion batteries. Solid-state batteries are highlighted for their improved energy density, faster charging times, and enhanced safety. QuantumScape is developing high-energy-density lithium metal batteries; Toyota and Samsung have introduced prototypes with remarkable range capabilities; and Solid Power is collaborating with major automotive companies. response:
# Solid-State Battery Technology for Electric Vehicles in 2024: Key Developments and Challenges

## Introduction

Solid-state battery technology emerged as a critical frontier in electric vehicle development during 2024, with researchers and manufacturers working to overcome the limitations of conventional lithium-ion batteries. According to a June 2024 publication in MRS Bulletin, solid-state batteries (SSBs) are "expected to play an important role in vehicle electrification within the next decade," with recent advances in materials, interfacial design, and manufacturing rapidly advancing SSB technologies toward commercialization (https://link.springer.com/article/10.1557/s43577-024-00740-7). The fundamental innovation involves replacing flammable liquid electrolytes with solid alternatives, which reduces risks of spontaneous combustion when batteries are damaged or overheat (https://techxplore.com/news/2026-03-electric-car-flames-solidstate-battery.html).

## Major Company Announcements and Breakthroughs

A significant milestone occurred when Donut Lab launched what they claimed as "the world's first all-solid-state battery in production vehicles," addressing key EV challenges including range, cost, charging speed, and dependence on rare-earth materials (https://www.linkedin.com/posts/aritulla_today-donut-lab-launched-the-donut-battery-activity-7414327842127355905-gjRM). The Donut Battery boasts impressive specifications: 400 Wh/kg energy density, lower cost than lithium-ion batteries, five-minute full charge capability, and design for up to 100,000 cycles using globally abundant materials. The first EV powered by this technology, the Verge TS Pro superbike, became available to customers, with Donut Lab working with multiple original equipment manufacturers on various applications from SUVs to stationary energy storage.

Meanwhile, QuantumScape continued advancing its anode-less cell design, which the company describes as "the industry's first," delivering high energy density while lowering material costs and simplifying manufacturing (https://www.quantumscape.com/). The company's lithium-metal solid-state batteries are designed to charge faster, go farther, last longer, and operate more safely than conventional EVs. QuantumScape achieved important validation when it delivered Alpha-2 solid-state prototypes to EV automakers and secured partnerships with Volkswagen's PowerCo, which confirmed that QuantumScape's solid-state cells passed initial endurance tests (https://www.quantumscape.com/newsroom/). A September 2024 industry report noted that major players including QuantumScape, Toyota, Solid Power, and Samsung SDI were engaged in significant research and development activities aimed at surpassing traditional lithium-ion battery limitations (https://seo.goover.ai/report/202409/go-public-report-en-9d78bdc4-5c4f-49af-90f5-acad52ae0302-0-0.html).

## Technical Challenges and Industry Perspectives

Despite these advances, substantial challenges remained for widespread adoption. The June 2024 MRS Bulletin article identified critical obstacles including "rate capability, reducing or eliminating the need for stack pressure," and high-temperature performance issues that must be addressed before mass commercialization (https://link.springer.com/article/10.1557/s43577-024-00740-7). Research published in scientific journals emphasized that while solid-state batteries offer potential for high energy density and improved safety compared to conventional lithium-ion batteries, "transitioning to industrial-scale manufacturing faces major hurdles," including critical challenges with electrolyte materials and production processes (https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0378775325021913). Catherine Sung of Prologium Technology, speaking at The Battery Show Asia in Hong Kong in March 2026, emphasized that the next generation of battery innovation must deliver improvements in safety, performance, and sustainability simultaneously, noting that "inorganic solid-state batteries remove the organic liquid electrolyte used in conventional lithium-ion batteries" (https://carbonwire.org/conversation/solid-state-batteries-could-accelerate-the-next-phase-of-the-energy-transition/).

## Conclusion

The solid-state battery landscape in 2024 represented a transitional period between laboratory breakthroughs and commercial viability. While companies like Donut Lab claimed production-ready systems and established players like QuantumScape advanced toward commercialization through automotive partnerships, the industry continued grappling with manufacturing scalability and performance optimization challenges. The technology's promise of enhanced safety through non-flammable solid electrolytes, combined with potential improvements in energy density and charging speed, positioned solid-state batteries as a crucial technology for the next phase of electric vehicle adoption, though widespread deployment remained dependent on overcoming significant technical and manufacturing hurdles.
